Description: Direct APIs to pull data directly from Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Slack, Teams without manual downloads.
Automatically extracting text from scanned image PDFs, JPEGs, and TIFFs so they become searchable.
Using the NSRL to automatically filter out junk system files (like Windows.dll) from forensic images.
Hashing files (MD5/SHA256) to instantly hide exact duplicates across the entire dataset.
Auto-generating searchable text transcripts from Zoom recordings, Microsoft Teams meetings, and voicemails.
Technology Assisted Review (Continuous Active Learning). The AI predicts how 50,000 documents should be tagged based on a lawyer tagging 500.
The AI reads the entire dataset and groups documents into thematic bubbles automatically.
A visual spider-web graph showing who emails whom the most, identifying hidden key players.
Grouping documents that are 80-99% similar (e.g., versions 1-10 of a contract draft) to review together.
Drawing black boxes over PII or privileged text directly in the viewer, burning the black box into exported files.
Automatically finding credit card numbers, SSNs, and medical data to suggest them for redaction.
Tagging 1,000 search results at once, or assigning batches of 500 documents to specific reviewers.
A graphical tree view of email conversations showing branches, forwards, and unique versus duplicative replies.
Stamping sequential, unique identifiers (e.g., SHERLOCK-0000001) on the bottom right corner of pages for opposing counsel.
Generating formal litigation-ready export packages containing Load Files (.DAT, .OPT), Native files, and TIFF images.
Tracking every single click, search, and view to maintain a defensible Chain of Custody.
Setting specific roles (e.g., Admin, Case Manager, Reviewer) so certain users can't delete or view highly confidential files.
